response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a broad web and full-stack background with some geospatial interest (GISCollective), but the role demands strong modern C++ or Rust for performance-critical routing pipeline work — neither of which appear meaningfully in his recent experience. His C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project, and it does not appear at all in his listed skills. His geospatial work is data-collection oriented rather than routing or navigation tile generation. The candidate's genuine strengths in TypeScript, Node.js, and event-driven architecture are not relevant to this position's core technical requirements.
strengths: Geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map GIS application shows genuine familiarity with geodata concepts
Distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second demonstrates understanding of high-throughput, low-latency systems
Strong track record with Linux environments and demonstrated comfort adapting across technical stacks and domains
concerns: No meaningful modern C++ or Rust experience — C++ is absent from his skills list and last used professionally over 10 years ago
No routing, navigation guidance, or tile generation experience; GIS work is limited to data collection platforms for NGOs, not production navigation pipelines
Currently on an open-ended career gap since October 2025, with no indication of C++/Rust upskilling during this period
